title: Implement PROP-072 — hoist Step 4c (PROP-066 helper invocation) to FIRST position in workspace-sync Step 4 collapsed bash block; primary reorder + secondary AGENT_NOTES self-attestation backstop; tinker self-applies
background:
PROP-072 (authored by tinker 2026-05-31T17:00Z in response to its own Mode 3 cost-engineering audit) identifies that PROP-066 + PROP-068 did NOT fully close the LLM Step 4c silent-skip defect. Empirical evidence: in the 7 workspace-sync cycles since PROP-068 deploy (09:33Z to 16:09Z), exactly ZERO PROP-066 step4c sentinels were committed, while all 7 cycles successfully ran Step 4a (FUSE→git commits visible) and Step 4b (run-reports visible). Tinker manually re-invoked the helper at 16:52Z and confirmed it works correctly (sentinel written, classification=equal, upstream_newer=true, action=pull-moved-auto-sync). The defect is positional: the LLM agent runs FIRST + LAST actions of the collapsed bash block reliably but drops MIDDLE actions.

Root cause hypothesis (PROP-072): the LLM is reliable at FIRST + LAST positions within a single bash block; Step 4c sits in the MIDDLE position (between 4a commit at top and 4b report+cleanup at bottom) and gets skipped. PROP-068's structural collapse closed one boundary class (trailing-section-header skip) but not the in-block middle-drop pattern.

Fix (PROP-072 primary): move the 4c sub-block to FIRST position immediately after the trap, so the position that's reliably executed IS the 4c invocation. The git→FUSE propagation 4c performs is independent of 4a's FUSE→git work; reordering is correctness-preserving.

Operator approved option 1 from cowork session: primary reorder + secondary AGENT_NOTES self-attestation backstop. Tinker self-applies (per PROP-072 Q4 explicit grant).

Q1_approve_primary_reorder.rationale: Empirical evidence is strong (7/7 cycles dropped Step 4c). FIRST-position reorder matches the observed pattern (top + bottom reliably execute). Reorder is correctness-preserving since 4c reads origin/main and 4a writes FUSE→git — order-independent operations on disjoint data.

Q2_approve_secondary_AGENT_NOTES_self_at.rationale: Cheap defense-in-depth (~3 lines). Turns future skip-events from silent-skip into visible-in-run-report. Worth shipping alongside primary so the next regression in this bug class is immediately observable rather than requiring tinker's periodic audit to detect.

Q3_approve_tertiary_step_0_startup_check.rationale: Primary + secondary should be sufficient. Tertiary startup-check (warn if prior sentinel >90min old) is belt-and-suspenders that adds complexity to Step 0 pre-flight. Hold off; can add later if primary + secondary insufficient.

Q4_tinker_self_apply_or_operator_commiss.rationale: Operator commission via directive (this file) granted. Pattern matches PROP-070 deploy earlier today (tinker self-applied per directive).

requested_work: Step 1 — Apply PROP-072 primary fix: in monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md Step 4 collapsed bash block, move the 4c sub-block (node sync-workspace-step4c.js helper invocation + git add sentinels + commit + push of any new sentinel files) from its current MIDDLE position to FIRST position IMMEDIATELY AFTER the bash-local trap setup. Keep all other ordering: trap → 4c FIRST → 4a (DOCS_NOTE + commit + push + rebase-retry) → FILES_COMMITTED capture → 4b (run-report write + commit + push) → rm $CLONE.

Step 2 — Update Step 4's prose 'Ordering inside the block matters' bullet list to reflect new order. Inline a comment in the prose explaining the correctness argument (4c reads origin/main, 4a writes FUSE→git, operations on disjoint data — reorder is correctness-preserving).

Step 3 — Apply PROP-072 secondary backstop: extend the AGENT_NOTES guidance comment in Step 4b to instruct the LLM to include the string 'step4c-not-invoked' in AGENT_NOTES if it observes Step 4c did not run this cycle (e.g., no sync-workspace-runs sentinel file created in this cycle's window). Tinker's soft-complaints grep already catches similar patterns; this makes the regression immediately visible in run-reports.

Step 4 — Validate via PROP-072 verification_pattern: `grep -q '4c FIRST per PROP-072' monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md && echo FIXED || echo STILL_BROKEN`. Expected output: FIXED. The marker '4c FIRST per PROP-072' should appear in the new ordering comment (inline or above the 4c block).
Step 5 — Commit + push the workspace-sync.md change in ONE commit. Commit message should reference DIRECTIVE-012 + PROP-072 + the FIRST/LAST/MIDDLE positional finding.

Step 6 — Manually copy workspace-sync.md to FUSE so the next scheduled workspace-sync cycle picks up the new ordering. Per the per-session FUSE staleness pattern documented in D31-010 (FND-01 + baby tracker observations); manual sync is the bootstrap path until PROP-071 lands.

Step 7 — Lifecycle: flip PROP-072.json status='implemented' with implementation_commit + implementation_at + implementation_run recorded. Flip this directive to status='completed' with prop_id_implemented='PROP-072'.

Step 8 — Post-deployment canary plan: (a) at next workspace-sync cycle (top of next hour), verify a sync-workspace-runs-<ts>.json sentinel is committed; (b) at the cycle AFTER that, verify a second sentinel — two sentinels in 2 cycles = primary fix confirmed working; (c) tinker Mode 2 quick-check on next non-directive run should grep for 'step4c-not-invoked' in workspace-sync run reports — expect 0 occurrences. If any signal absent within 2 cycles, file follow-up directive for PROP-072 tertiary backstop + investigation of additional skip-by-omission positions.
non_goals: Do NOT apply tertiary Step 0 startup-check in this run — operator deferred per Q3 answer above. Hold off pending primary + secondary effectiveness measurement.
Do NOT bump workspace-sync cadence in this run — PROP-072 is a correctness fix, not a throughput fix.

Do NOT touch PROP-066's helper script (monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js) — it works correctly when invoked (proven by tinker's manual 16:52Z test). The defect is purely in the prompt's invocation point.
Do NOT touch PROP-068's collapsed-block architecture — the collapse is still correct; only the in-block ordering needs to change.

Do NOT supersede PROP-066 or PROP-068. PROP-072 is a refinement of PROP-068's ordering, not a replacement. PROP-066's helper script remains the primary git→FUSE sync mechanism; PROP-072 just ensures it actually gets invoked.

context_pointers.predecessor_audit: tinker-2026-05-31T17-00 Mode 3 cost-engineering audit FND-01 (the audit that surfaced the recurrence)
context_pointers.prop_066: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-066-step-4c-helper-script-extraction.json (implemented 2026-05-31T08:42Z, commit ba212cf) — helper script is correct; invocation point is the bug
context_pointers.prop_068: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-068-collapse-cleanup-into-step-4b.json (implemented 2026-05-31T09:33Z, commit e22b0e8) — collapse is correct; in-block ordering is the bug
context_pointers.manual_invocation_test_evidence: monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-runs-2026-05-31T16-52-42Z.json — proof that the helper works when invoked
context_pointers.empirical_skip_evidence: 7 workspace-sync run reports between 09:33Z and 16:09Z; 0 sync-workspace-runs sentinels in same window. ratio = 0/7 = 100% skip rate.

expected_output: Single commit on origin/main containing: monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md (4c hoisted to FIRST position + AGENT_NOTES self-attestation backstop comment + ordering prose updated). PLUS: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-072-*.json status flipped to 'implemented' + implementation_commit recorded. PLUS: this directive flipped to status='completed' with prop_id_implemented='PROP-072'. PLUS: workspace-sync.md FUSE-bootstrap-synced.
estimated_effort: ~20-30 min Opus. Reorder is mechanical (~10 lines moved); AGENT_NOTES comment addition is ~3 lines; prose update is ~5 lines.
priority_note: HIGH — affects every workspace-sync cycle currently producing zero PROP-066 sentinels. Fixing this restores the git→FUSE sync the operator was depending on (and the integrity-side FUSE-staleness warnings have been complaining about). Sequenced ahead of any PROP-071 (FUSE-staleness audit) follow-up — fixing the producer (workspace-sync Step 4c) is upstream of the consumers PROP-071 audits.
